commit.message:
chore: switch news aggregation model from opus to haiku 4.5 (#9)

Replaces claude-opus-4-6 with claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 for the
aggregateNewsStories function to reduce cost and latency.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace predict/predict_zero_shot with unified predict() → _predict() pattern (#360)

* feat: add predict() guard in base class, rename TTM predict -> _predict

- Base class predict() now validates is_fitted + supports_zero_shot
  before delegating to abstract _predict() (sktime pattern)
- TTM _predict() branches on is_fitted: fine-tuned uses preprocessor
  pipeline, zero-shot uses RevIN-only pipeline
- Remove standalone predict_zero_shot() from TTM
- Update all workflow scripts and tests to use predict() directly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: rename predict -> _predict in all remaining model classes

Complete the base class predict() guard pattern across all models.
Base class predict() validates is_fitted + supports_zero_shot, then
delegates to abstract _predict(). Removes redundant is_fitted guards
from Moirai and Moment (base class handles it now).

Models renamed: Chronos2, TimesFM, Sundial, Moirai, Moment, TimeGrad, TiDE

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove dead predict_zero_shot methods and update docs

- Remove predict_zero_shot() from TimesFM (was just self.predict() wrapper)
- Remove predict_zero_shot() from Chronos2 (lazy zero-shot predictor)
- Fix Chronos2 error message that referenced predict_zero_shot()
- Update TTM README.md and flowchart.md to reflect unified predict() API

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: replace AutoGluon zero-shot workaround with Chronos2Pipeline

Zero-shot inference previously required creating a temporary
TimeSeriesPredictor, fabricating dummy training data, and calling
fit(fine_tune_steps=0) just to load pretrained weights. This was
an AutoGluon API limitation workaround.

Replace with Chronos2Pipeline.from_pretrained() + predict() from the
chronos-forecasting library — same pattern already used in
scripts/regenerate_quantile_8h.py and scripts/test_chronos2_api.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use predict_quantiles instead of predict for Chronos2 zero-shot

Chronos-2 (Bolt) is deterministic — predict() doesn't accept
num_samples (that's Chronos-1's sampling API). Use predict_quantiles()
which returns (quantiles, mean) directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: reshape context tensor to 3D for Chronos2Pipeline

Chronos-2 expects (n_series, n_variates, history_length), not (1, length).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: squeeze extra dimension from predict_quantiles output

predict_quantiles returns (1, forecast_length) per series; squeeze
to get the 1D array the workflow expects.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback

- Error message: mention load() alongside fit()
- Add unit tests for predict guard (4 states: fitted/unfitted × zero-shot/no)
- Remove return_dict from TTM _predict (predict always returns np.ndarray)
- Fix CLI help text: "all are used for eval" not "first is used for eval"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detach and move to CPU before numpy conversion

Pipeline runs on CUDA; .numpy() fails on GPU tensors without
.detach().cpu() first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove return_dict from Moirai and Moment _predict

predict() always returns np.ndarray. Removes return_dict from Moirai
and Moment (same cleanup already applied to TTM). Cleans up unused
Union/Dict imports and base class docstring references.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove stale test_predict_before_fit from Chronos2 tests

Chronos2Forecaster.supports_zero_shot=True means predict() before fit
is now valid (zero-shot inference). The predict guard is tested
comprehensively in test_predict_guard.py. Also fixes stale docstring
reference to evaluate() override.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove duplicate predict() entry in TTM README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* The call to self.supports_lora() should have been self.supports_lora because this is a property

* Some fixes to chronos2 tests.

* Smal fix to TTM.

---------

feat: smooth swipe navigation, splash logo update, backend warmup

- MainTabsScreen: Instagram-style PageView with Favorites/Home/CreateListing
- BottomNav synced with PageView, conditional swipe lock on CreateListing step 1+
- Auth check on swipe to CreateListing tab
- Splash: app_logo.png replaces CC text, black bg & cypcar text removed
- Backend warmup on splash initState for instant home screen load

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

⚡ perf: Phase 2+3 — parallel fetch + explicit columns (useEventos + useChamados)

useEventos:
- COUNT HEAD request → parallel batches (PARALLEL_LIMIT=6)
- Dual parallel COUNT for recentes + vencidos
- Explicit ESSENTIAL_COLUMNS (avoids transferring unused fields)
- Sequential fallback if COUNT fails

useChamados:
- COUNT HEAD request → parallel batches (PARALLEL_LIMIT=6)
- Explicit CHAMADOS_COLUMNS replacing select("*")
- Sequential fallback if COUNT fails

Expected improvement: 10–15x faster initial load for large ISPs.

commit.message:
feat: multi-policy selection & schema registry improvements (#182, #181) (#211)

* feat(relay): PolicyRegistry with multi-policy resolve (#182)

Add PolicyRegistry type supporting multi-policy selection. Contracts
declare which enforcement policy governs them via enforcement_policy_hash;
unknown hashes get PolicyNotAvailable, omitted hashes fall back to the
configured default with a warning on multi-policy relays.

- PolicyRegistry: new(), single(), dev_skip(), resolve(), hashes()
- ResolvedPolicy<'a> wrapper for resolved hash + policy ref
- load_all_policies() helper loads and validates all lockfile entries
- PolicyNotAvailable and NoPolicyResolvable error variants
- AppState: replace enforcement_policy + enforcement_policy_hash with
  policy_registry
- main.rs: multi-policy startup with AV_DEFAULT_POLICY_HASH env var,
  fail-closed when ambiguous
- relay_core: resolve policy first, wire through to receipt building
- build_receipt_v2 / build_failure_receipt_v2: accept policy_hash param
- Health endpoint: loaded_policy_hashes field for monitoring
- All integration tests updated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(relay): schema deprecation warning + discovery endpoints (#181, #182)

- Add tracing::warn when contracts provide both inline output_schema
  and output_schema_hash (inline is deprecated, use hash-only ref)
- Add available_schema_hashes and available_policy_hashes to
  /capabilities endpoint for client-facing discovery
- SchemaRegistry::hashes() now returns sorted results
- Schema resolution tests pinning the three-branch behaviour

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(client): schema-ref contract builder (#181)

Add buildRelayContractWithSchemaRef() that produces contracts with a
stub output_schema ({}) and output_schema_hash for registry lookup.
Supports optional schemaHash and policyHash overrides.

This is a VFC-compatibility shim — the clean path is making
output_schema optional in the VFC Contract type (separate PR).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style(relay): cargo fmt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(relay): remove redundant sort, add receipt-binding tests

- Remove redundant available_schema_hashes.sort() in capabilities_handler
  (SchemaRegistry::hashes() already returns sorted)
- Add 3 receipt-binding tests for multi-policy correctness:
  - selected policy in receipt matches requested, not default
  - preflight and receipt agree on policy_hash
  - default vs explicit paths produce different policy hashes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Add scripts/ to .gitignore

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Surface real server errors in UI; fix ensureUserRow and add NOT NULL defaults

- api.ts: All data functions now read server error message instead of
  throwing generic strings, so the real DB/RLS error is visible in the toast
- ServiceHistory.tsx: Show actual error message instead of "Failed to save"
- routes.ts ensureUserRow: Now THROWS on critical failures instead of
  silently swallowing, so FK inserts don't run when user row creation fails
- routes.ts: All createUser calls (login, register, verify-email, profile
  PATCH, ensureUserRow) now include explicit NOT NULL column defaults
  (subscription_tier, role, two_factor_enabled, profile_completed) to
  prevent constraint violations if DB doesn't apply defaults
- service-history POST: Bypass Zod parse (avoids Date/string type mismatch),
  build snake_case row directly with verbose logging

Add warning when campaign overwrites existing daily actuals (rail transfer)

that will overwrite existing daily actuals:

ORCH_002 COMPLETED: BUG-9 gradient clipping diagnosis

- Grad norm stats: mean=14.5, median=8.9, 0% under 0.5 threshold
- 8 regression sub-losses dominate gradient magnitude
- Recommendation: raise max_norm from 0.5 to 10.0 (55.7% unclipped)
- Options B (loss scaling) and C (per-layer clip) not recommended
- Full report in shared/logs/report_ORCH_002.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: always use by-ID endpoints for visa submit, never fallback to non-ID paths

Root cause: users without ParticipantUser records hit the non-by-ID
submit/update endpoints which require event_id through _resolve_visa_context.
Even though event_id is passed, cached old frontend JS may not include it.
Now the submit flow always uses updateMyVisaById + submitMyVisaById,
eliminating the fallback path entirely. If _visaId is null (shouldn't happen),
shows a clear error instead of silently failing.

commit.message:
fix: inject ContentManager via @Environment for reliable @Observable tracking

Accessing a global singleton from SwiftUI body doesn't reliably set up
observation subscriptions. Inject ContentManager.shared via .environment()
at the app root and read it with @Environment(ContentManager.self) in
ContentBrowserView, SplitLayoutView, and ContentCard so SwiftUI properly
invalidates those views when importedContent changes after a download.

commit.message:
Fix: portail résident — PINs démo passent à 6 chiffres (100000+idx)

Le formulaire avait pattern="[0-9]{6}" qui bloquait la soumission
pour les PINs 4 chiffres (1000+idx) au niveau de la validation HTML5.
Passage à String(100000+idx) dans mockData.js et getResidentPortalPin.
Ajout d'un indice démo discret (NRWST-2026 / 100001) dans LoginPage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: real UK data sources — Police.uk crime data and Postcodes.io geocoding

Reports now use real data instead of AI estimates. Before calling Claude, the
system geocodes the area via Postcodes.io (lat/lng, council, ward, region,
constituency) and fetches 3 months of street-level crime data from Police.uk.
Real data is injected into the prompt so Claude analyses facts, not guesses.

- Postcodes.io integration: postcode lookup, place search, reverse geocoding
- Police.uk integration: street-level crimes, category breakdown, top streets,
  monthly trends, outcome analysis (last 3 months)
- Prompt engineering: real data block injected with explicit instructions to
  use exact figures; Safety section must reference actual police.uk stats
- Data sources badge on report footer (green neon tags: "postcodes.io", "police.uk")
- AreaReport type updated with optional data_sources field
- Graceful degradation: if APIs fail, report still generates with estimates

Partially closes AR-5 (Police.uk + Postcodes.io done; ONS, Google Places, OSM remain)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Expand synonym dictionaries (620 entries) + fingerprint rarity filter + docs

Dictionary expansion:
- Latin: 222 new synonym entries across 51 semantic fields (verbs of
  destruction, motion, speech, perception, emotion, physical action)
- Greek: 111 entries, 27 fields (Homeric/tragic vocabulary)
- Greek-Latin cross-lingual: 289 entries, 36 fields (~120 verbal)
- V3 originals archived in backend/synonymy/originals_v3/ with checksums
- Full documentation in DICTIONARY_ADDITIONS_REFERENCE.md
- Fixed conrumpo/corrumpo lemma mismatch (UD treebank inconsistency)

Structural fingerprint noise reduction:
- Added fingerprint rarity filter (MAX_FINGERPRINT_FREQ=4): skip patterns
  appearing in >4 lines across source+target texts. Cuts ~50% of noise
  fingerprint pairs while preserving distinctive matches like the Thomas
  tricolon (Georg 3.481/DRN 6.1140, pattern freq=2)
- Raised semantic recovery threshold from 0.5 to 0.55

Thomas tricolon rank: ~245 → 25 in Georgics 3 × DRN 6

Benchmark recall unchanged: 800/862 (92.8%) across all 5 pairs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add wikidocs plugin — GitHub wiki as docs/ submodule

New plugin with two commands:
- /wiki-init: auto-creates the GitHub wiki, adds it as a submodule at docs/,
  configures rebase-on-pull and merge=ours for cross-branch sync
- /wiki-sync: pulls latest wiki (rebase), commits local changes, pushes,
  updates submodule ref in parent repo

Includes PostToolUse hooks to nudge docs into docs/ and remind about
wiki-sync after commits.

commit.message:
Optimize IsingFit/MGM: pre-flatten data, use glmnetPathFlat

- Pre-flatten data to Float64Array(n*p) once, build flat sub-matrices
  per node regression instead of n*p small number[] allocations
- Use glmnetPathFlat from carm to skip number[][] → Float64Array
  conversion inside glmnet
- MGM: skip data copy when no gaussian columns need scaling
- Tighten R equivalence tolerances (0.15→0.02 weights, 0.2→0.05 thresholds)

fix: route KuCoin through proxy — KuCoin removed public CORS headers

Direct browser fetch to api.kucoin.com now returns 200 but no
Access-Control-Allow-Origin header, causing CORS block.
Route through proxy like all other exchanges; returns null silently
if Oracle Cloud IP is blocked by KuCoin.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Perf: Ajustar batch_size de 3000 a 2000

Reducción para mejor estabilidad en MySQL remoto:
- Más batches pero más manejables
- Menor riesgo de lock timeout
- Mejor recuperación de errores

Cambios:
• Products INSERT: batch_size 3000 → 2000
• Products UPDATE: batch_size 3000 → 2000
• Customers: batch_size 3000 → 2000

Batches para 52,686 productos:
• batch_size=1000: ~53 batches
• batch_size=2000: ~27 batches ✓
• batch_size=3000: ~18 batches
• batch_size=4000: ~14 batches
• batch_size=5000: ~11 batches

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
